─[ LXC GPU Passthrough (NVIDIA) ]─ ┐

name: lxc-gpu-passthrough description: NVIDIA GPU passthrough to LXC containers for transcoding user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob

LXC GPU Passthrough (NVIDIA)

Context

Passing through NVIDIA GPUs to LXC containers for hardware transcoding or compute (CUDA) in Proxmox.

Snags

Architecture Compatibility

Kepler (GTX 650 Ti Boost and similar): Unstable for NVENC transcoding. Playback freezes and app crashes. Use CPU (libx264) for these older cards. Pascal and newer (GTX 1050+) are reliable.

Driver Version Match

The userspace driver version inside the LXC container MUST exactly match the host driver version. Fix: Install inside LXC with --no-kernel-module:

./NVIDIA-Linux-x86_64-<version>.run --no-kernel-module --silent --no-questions

Podman Mounting

Docker 29.x is incompatible with LXC. Use Podman. Passing GPUs to Podman containers inside LXC requires mounting all nvidia device nodes and specific library files with -v.

Workaround

LXC Config Addition

Add to /etc/pve/lxc/<vmid>.conf:

# GPU device access (major 195 and 236)
lxc.cgroup2.devices.allow: c 195:* rwm
lxc.cgroup2.devices.allow: c 236:* rwm
# Bind-mount device nodes
lxc.mount.entry: /dev/nvidia0 dev/nvidia0 none bind,optional,create=file
lxc.mount.entry: /dev/nvidiactl dev/nvidiactl none bind,optional,create=file
lxc.mount.entry: /dev/nvidia-uvm dev/nvidia-uvm none bind,optional,create=file
lxc.mount.entry: /dev/nvidia-uvm-tools dev/nvidia-uvm-tools none bind,optional,create=file

Podman Forwarding

NETAVARK inside LXC may block NEW connections. Use this iptables rule on the host or inside the LXC:

iptables -I NETAVARK_FORWARD 1 -d <container-subnet> -m conntrack --ctstate NEW -j ACCEPT

Diagnosis

  • LXC Level: nvidia-smi should show the GPU in the container.
  • Container Level: podman exec <container> ffmpeg -hwaccels should show cuda.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
